title: Build and Test an MCP Server with Official SDKs · Mastery Check
summary: Implement a typed server with tools, resources, and automated protocol tests. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a runnable tested MCP server. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Select an official maintained SDK and pin a compatible protocol target.
Create server metadata, capabilities, handlers, and transport entry points.
Validate structured inputs and return typed content or structured errors.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Local MCP protocol and tool-contract portfolio."""

# Add typed, bounded protocol increments below.

# Skill and MCP integration portfolio
# Record scope, triggers, permissions, tests, and maintenance evidence.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Sdk Selection evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Sdk Selection assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Select an official maintained SDK and pin a compatible protocol target. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: agent ext server build. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Sdk Selection, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a runnable tested MCP server.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Any abandoned wrapper is equivalent to an official SDK. This fails the Sdk Selection contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: OpenAI Codex Manual: Build Skills (Verified current 2026-07-13) — Controls Codex skill structure, progressive disclosure, locations, invocation, plugins, and dependencies.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Select an official maintained SDK and pin a compatible protocol target.

Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Inspector Debug applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use protocol inspection and client logs to diagnose negotiation and transport failures.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
